title: Process Flow of the 'su' Command
description: A flowchart illustrating the sequence of actions and decisions performed by the UNIX 'su' (substitute user) command when a user attempts to switch to another account.
relationships: User invokes 'su [username]' which triggers the kernel to check if the current user has the necessary permissions. If permissions are sufficient, the Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) framework performs authentication (e.g., prompts for password). Upon successful authentication, a new process is spawned, the environment is modified (e.g., HOME, USER variables changed), and finally the target user's login shell is executed.
